claims.text: c.1935C>A (p.D645E) was detected in all cases of IOPD (5 cases) identified in the Taiwan NBS program covering 191,786 newborns screened by MS/MS.

citations.quote: GAA gene analysis demonstrated that c.1935C>A (p.D645E) was detected in all cases of IOPD, c.[752C>T; 761C>T] (p.[S251L; S254L]) was detected in 8 cases of LOPD, and the c.1726G>A (p.G576S) pseudodeficiency variant was detected in 2 cases of IOPD and 5 cases of LOPD.

claims.text: c.1935C>A is listed as one of the frequently detected pathogenic variants in the Taiwan NBS program covering 473,738 newborns (Chiang et al. 2012), which identified 9 IOPD and 19 LOPD patients.
c.1935C>A is listed as one of the frequently detected pathogenic variants in the Taiwan NBS program covering 191,786 newborns (Liao et al. 2014), which identified 5 IOPD and 11 LOPD patients.
c.1935C>A (p.D645E) is described as a common missense variant in Taiwan associated with IOPD.

citations.quote: c.1935C>A (p.D645E), a common missense variant in Taiwan [6], and c.2560C>T (p.R854*), a common nonsense variant in Africa [57], are considered variants for IOPD.
